title: L09 · Preterite vs Imperfect — Two Past Tenses
objective: Spanish has TWO simple past tenses where English has one. The PRETERITE = a finished event with a clear boundary. The IMPERFECT = an ongoing past situation, habit, or background. Knowing which to use is the single biggest A2 challenge.
strategy: Three parts: (1) learn the preterite forms. (2) learn the imperfect forms (easier — only 3 irregulars). (3) drill the CONTRAST in section 4 — these example pairs make the distinction click. French speakers have a HUGE advantage: it's the same distinction as passé composé vs imparfait.

sections.title: Preterite (pretérito indefinido) — completed events
sections.note: Used for actions that HAPPENED and FINISHED. 'I ate', 'we went', 'she said'. The corresponding French tense is the passé composé.

blocks.audioText: hablé, hablaste, habló
blocks.rule: -AR endings: -é, -aste, -ó, -amos, -asteis, -aron. Note accents on yo and él forms.

examples.translation: Yesterday I spoke with my mother. / Hier, j'ai parlé avec ma mère.
Did you speak with him? / Lui as-tu parlé?

blocks.audioText: comí, comiste, comió
blocks.rule: -ER and -IR share the same preterite endings: -í, -iste, -ió, -imos, -isteis, -ieron. Accents on yo and él.

examples.translation: I ate fish last night. / J'ai mangé du poisson hier soir.

blocks.audioText: viví, viviste, vivió

examples.translation: I lived in Madrid for two years. / J'ai vécu à Madrid pendant deux ans.

blocks.audioText: fui, fuiste, fue, fuimos, fuisteis, fueron
blocks.translation: to be / to go (preterite) / être / aller
blocks.rule: TRAP: SER and IR share the SAME preterite. Context tells you which. 'Fui a Madrid' = I went. 'Fui feliz' = I was happy.

examples.translation: I went to Madrid in July. (ir) / Je suis allé à Madrid en juillet.
It was a good party. (ser) / Ce fut / C'était une bonne fête.

blocks.audioText: tuve, tuviste, tuvo
blocks.rule: Irregular stem changes to 'tuv-' for all forms. Endings: -e, -iste, -o, -imos, -isteis, -ieron (note no accents!).

examples.translation: I had a problem yesterday. / J'ai eu un problème hier.

blocks.audioText: hice, hiciste, hizo
blocks.rule: Stem 'hic-' but the él form is 'hizo' (z, not c — to keep the /θ/ sound before 'o').

examples.translation: What did you do yesterday? / Qu'as-tu fait hier?

blocks.audioText: dije, dijiste, dijo
blocks.rule: Verbs with stem ending in 'j' (dij-, traj-, conduj-) DROP the i in the ellos form: 'dijeron' NOT 'dijieron'.

examples.translation: He told me the truth. / Il m'a dit la vérité.

sections.title: Imperfect (pretérito imperfecto) — ongoing past
sections.note: Used for past habits, descriptions, ongoing actions. 'I used to live', 'she was reading', 'it was cold'. French equivalent: imparfait.

blocks.audioText: hablaba, hablabas, hablaba
blocks.translation: I used to speak / was speaking — je parlais
blocks.rule: -AR endings: -aba, -abas, -aba, -ábamos, -abais, -aban. The 'b' in the stem is characteristic. yo and él forms are IDENTICAL — context tells you which.

examples.text: De niño, hablaba mucho con mi abuela.
examples.audioText: De niño, hablaba mucho con mi abuela.
examples.translation: As a child, I used to talk a lot with my grandma. / Enfant, je parlais beaucoup avec ma grand-mère.

blocks.audioText: comía, comías, comía; vivía, vivías, vivía
blocks.translation: I used to eat / live — je mangeais / je vivais
blocks.rule: -ER and -IR share the same imperfect endings: -ía, -ías, -ía, -íamos, -íais, -ían. The í always carries an accent.

examples.translation: We used to live in the countryside. / Nous vivions à la campagne.

blocks.audioText: era, eras, era, éramos, erais, eran
blocks.translation: I was, used to be — j'étais
blocks.rule: ONE of only three irregular imperfects. Memorise this — used for past descriptions all the time.

examples.translation: It was a sunny day. / C'était une journée ensoleillée. — description in past

blocks.audioText: iba, ibas, iba, íbamos, ibais, iban
blocks.translation: I used to go / was going — j'allais

examples.text: Iba al parque todos los domingos.
examples.audioText: Iba al parque todos los domingos.
examples.translation: I used to go to the park every Sunday. / J'allais au parc tous les dimanches.

blocks.audioText: veía, veías, veía, veíamos, veíais, veían
blocks.translation: I used to see / was seeing — je voyais
blocks.rule: Third irregular: keeps the full 've-' stem. Otherwise normal -ía endings.

examples.translation: We used to watch films on Saturdays. / Nous regardions des films le samedi.

sections.title: When to use which — the rules
sections.note: Don't try to translate from English ('I lived' vs 'I was living'). Think about whether the action is BOUNDED (finished) or UNBOUNDED (ongoing/habit). For French speakers: same rules as passé composé vs imparfait.

blocks.text: Preterite: a finished event with a CLEAR endpoint or duration
blocks.audioText: Comí a las dos. Viví en Madrid tres años. Ayer fui al cine.
blocks.rule: Use preterite when the action is over and you know when/for how long. Trigger words: ayer (yesterday), anoche (last night), la semana pasada (last week), en 2020, una vez (once), de repente (suddenly).

examples.translation: Yesterday I went to the cinema. (one finished event) / Hier, je suis allé au cinéma.
I studied Spanish for two years. (defined duration, finished) / J'ai étudié l'espagnol pendant deux ans.
In 2020 I moved to Spain. / En 2020, j'ai déménagé en Espagne.

blocks.text: Imperfect: ongoing past, habits, descriptions, age in the past
blocks.audioText: Tenía cinco años. Vivía en el campo. Llovía mucho.
blocks.rule: Use imperfect for: (1) habits ('I used to'). (2) descriptions in the past ('It was sunny'). (3) telling AGE in the past ('I was 5'). (4) background actions while something else happened. Trigger words: siempre (always), a menudo (often), todos los días (every day), de niño (as a child), mientras (while).

examples.text: Cuando era niño, tenía un perro.
examples.audioText: Cuando era niño, tenía un perro.
examples.translation: When I was a child, I had a dog. / Quand j'étais enfant, j'avais un chien.

examples.text: Siempre íbamos a la playa en verano.
examples.audioText: Siempre íbamos a la playa en verano.
examples.translation: We always used to go to the beach in summer. / Nous allions toujours à la plage en été.
It was cold and it was raining. (description) / Il faisait froid et il pleuvait.

sections.title: The crucial contrast — same situation, different choice
sections.note: These pairs make the distinction click. Notice how the SAME English verb maps to different Spanish tenses depending on bounded vs unbounded.

blocks.text: Ongoing background vs single interrupting event
blocks.audioText: Caminaba por la calle cuando vi a María.
blocks.rule: Set the scene with imperfect, drop in the event with preterite. Classic 'I was X-ing when Y happened' structure.

examples.text: Caminaba por la calle cuando vi a María.
examples.audioText: Caminaba por la calle cuando vi a María.
examples.translation: I was walking down the street when I saw Maria. / Je marchais dans la rue quand j'ai vu Maria.
examples.ipa: Walking = ongoing (imperfect). Seeing Maria = single moment (preterite).

examples.translation: I was eating when the phone rang. / Je mangeais quand le téléphone a sonné.

blocks.audioText: Iba al parque cada domingo. Fui al parque el domingo pasado.
blocks.rule: If it was habitual, use imperfect. If it was a specific instance, use preterite. The word 'cada/siempre/todos los X' signals imperfect; 'el X pasado/ayer/anoche' signals preterite.

examples.text: De pequeño, iba al parque cada domingo.
examples.audioText: De pequeño, iba al parque cada domingo.
examples.translation: As a kid, I'd go to the park every Sunday. (habit) / Petit, j'allais au parc tous les dimanches.

examples.text: El domingo pasado, fui al parque.
examples.audioText: El domingo pasado, fui al parque.
examples.translation: Last Sunday, I went to the park. (one time) / Dimanche dernier, je suis allé au parc.

blocks.audioText: Era feliz. De repente me puse triste.
blocks.rule: 'Era feliz' = I WAS happy (state, description). 'Me puse triste' = I BECAME sad (event, transition). State = imperfect. Change = preterite.

examples.text: Era feliz hasta que me enteré.
examples.audioText: Era feliz hasta que me enteré.
examples.translation: I was happy until I found out. / J'étais heureux jusqu'à ce que je l'apprenne.

examples.text: Hacía sol pero de repente empezó a llover.
examples.audioText: Hacía sol pero de repente empezó a llover.
examples.translation: It was sunny but suddenly it started to rain. / Il faisait soleil mais soudain il s'est mis à pleuvoir.

blocks.text: Knew (had the knowledge — imp) vs found out (acquired it — pret)
blocks.audioText: Sabía la respuesta. Supe la verdad ayer.
blocks.rule: Some verbs change meaning between the two tenses. CRITICAL ONES: saber, conocer, querer, poder.

examples.translation: I knew the answer. (had the knowledge) / Je savais la réponse.
I found out the truth yesterday. (acquired knowledge — single moment) / J'ai appris la vérité hier.

examples.text: Conocía a Juan desde hace años.
examples.audioText: Conocía a Juan desde hace años.
examples.translation: I had known Juan for years. (ongoing acquaintance)

examples.text: Conocí a Juan en una fiesta.
examples.audioText: Conocí a Juan en una fiesta.
examples.translation: I met Juan at a party. (first encounter — single event) / J'ai rencontré Juan à une fête.
I didn't want to go. (didn't feel like) / Je ne voulais pas y aller.
I refused to go. (actively declined) / J'ai refusé d'y aller.

sections.title: A short story — both tenses in action
sections.note: Watch how the imperfect sets the scene and the preterite advances the plot.

blocks.text: Era una noche fría de diciembre.
blocks.audioText: Era una noche fría de diciembre.
blocks.translation: It was a cold December night. / C'était une nuit froide de décembre. — description, imp

blocks.text: Yo estaba en casa y leía un libro.
blocks.audioText: Yo estaba en casa y leía un libro.
blocks.translation: I was at home and I was reading a book. / J'étais à la maison et je lisais un livre. — ongoing, imp

blocks.text: De repente, alguien tocó la puerta.
blocks.audioText: De repente, alguien tocó la puerta.
blocks.translation: Suddenly, someone knocked at the door. / Soudain, quelqu'un a frappé à la porte. — event, pret

blocks.audioText: Me levanté y abrí.
blocks.translation: I got up and opened it. / Je me suis levé et j'ai ouvert. — sequential events, pret

blocks.text: Era mi viejo amigo Pablo, a quien no veía desde hace años.
blocks.audioText: Era mi viejo amigo Pablo, a quien no veía desde hace años.
blocks.translation: It was my old friend Pablo, whom I hadn't seen in years. / C'était mon vieil ami Pablo, que je n'avais pas vu depuis des années. — both imp (description + state of not seeing)
